message:
fix(google-sheets): validate base_url + bind capability token to spreadsheet URIs

Mirrors the google-docs hardening across the google-sheets connector:
adds the same `validate_sheets_base_url` guard on `configure`, stamps
canonical `google-sheets:spreadsheet:{id}` resource URIs on each
invoke, and routes verification through `verify_bound` so a capability
token can't cross documents. Also adds a `SheetsClient::with_base_url`
seam (parallel to `DocsClient::with_base_url`) for deterministic
tests.

## SheetsClient::with_base_url

New `pub fn with_base_url(mut self, base_url: impl Into<String>)
-> Self`. Trims the trailing slash and overwrites `self.base_url`.
`#[must_use]` to surface accidental drop of the return value.

## base_url validation

`validate_sheets_base_url(raw)` has the same shape as its docs
counterpart and enforces the same policy (scheme http/https; http
only for localhost/127.0.0.1/::1; no userinfo; no query; no
fragment; host must match `googleapis.com` exact or
`.googleapis.com` suffix), returning
`FcpError::InvalidRequest { code: 1003, ... }` on violation. The
canonicalized trimmed string is then handed to
`SheetsClient::with_base_url(...)`.

Helpers `is_local_test_host`, `host_is_googleapis` are identical to
the docs version (kept per-connector so each crate can be vendored
independently without a shared dependency).

## Resource URIs + verify_bound

`SheetsConnector::handle_invoke` is reshaped to:

1. Parse the operation into an `OperationId` (1002 on failure).
2. Look up the declared capability string for the operation via
   introspection; unknown operations fail with
   `FcpError::OperationNotGranted { operation }` *before* any token
   parsing.
3. Require `client` and `verifier` (both → `NotConfigured`).
4. Require + parse `params["capability_token"]` (missing → 1001,
   unparseable → 1001).
5. Build `resource_uris_for_operation(operation, &input)`:

       sheets.get_spreadsheet
       sheets.get_values
       sheets.update_values
       sheets.append_values
       sheets.clear_values
           → ["google-sheets:spreadsheet:{spreadsheet_id}"]

   All other operations → `vec![]`. The helper calls
   `require_str(input, "spreadsheet_id")` so an invoke missing
   the id fails with `InvalidRequest` before reaching the verifier.
6. Call `verifier.verify_bound(token, &cap_id, &op_id,
   &resource_uris)`.

Also switches the configured-flag plumbing from the raw
`self.base.configured.store(true, Relaxed)` to the dedicated
`self.base.set_configured(true)` helper so the sheets connector uses
the same API every other connector does.

## Tests

Eight new unit tests cover the new code with the same shape as the
docs tests, exercising foreign-host rejection, substring smuggle
(`https://evil.com/sheets.googleapis.com/v4`),
query/fragment/userinfo rejection, localhost http allowance,
lookalike host rejection, and the
`sheets.{get_spreadsheet,update_values}` → resource URI mapping.

`invoke_unknown_operation_is_denied_before_token_validation`
replaces the prior `invoke_unknown_operation`, matching the
configure-free shape used by other connectors and asserting the
error is `OperationNotGranted` rather than
`InvalidRequest { code: 1002 }`.
